headlines.text: Israel and Iran trade missile fire on war's 100th day, threatening fragile ceasefire
Apple WWDC kicks off: Tim Cook's final keynote unveils Gemini-powered Siri and iOS 27
7.8 magnitude earthquake strikes Mindanao, Philippines; at least 35 dead, tsunami warnings issued
Trump demands Israel and Iran 'immediately stop shooting' as peace talks hang by a thread
National gas average at $4.17/gallon as Iran war keeps oil markets on edge
Stocks rebound Monday: Nasdaq jumps ~1.4% as chip stocks recover and Middle East tensions ease slightly
2026 Tony Awards hosted by Pink at Radio City Music Hall; Aubrey Plaza and Christopher Abbott debut as couple
Deb Haaland wins historic Democratic primary in New Mexico Senate race
Pope Leo XIV addresses Spanish parliament, calls for 'moral renewal' in public life
Bitcoin rebounds above $63,500 after dipping below $60K over the weekend

breaking.text: BREAKING: Israel and Iran exchange missile strikes on war's 100th day — both sides now pausing fire after Trump intervenes

stories.hed: Israel and Iran Trade Strikes in Worst Escalation Since April Ceasefire
stories.dek: On the war's 100th day, Israel bombed Iranian petrochemical facilities and air defenses while Iran launched ~30 ballistic missiles at Israeli military bases. Both sides have now paused fire — after Trump posted on Truth Social demanding they stop, which apparently still works.

stories.hed: 7.8 Earthquake Devastates Southern Philippines, Killing at Least 35
stories.dek: A powerful offshore quake struck Mindanao on the first day of school, collapsing buildings in General Santos City and triggering tsunami warnings across the region. Over 130 aftershocks have followed — because apparently the earth had more to say.

stories.hed: Apple WWDC 2026: Tim Cook's Final Keynote Unveils Gemini-Powered Siri and iOS 27
stories.dek: In what is Tim Cook's 15th and final WWDC as CEO, Apple announced a total rebuild of Siri using Google's Gemini AI and previewed six new OS platforms. Siri can now hold real conversations — only 15 years after it promised it could.

stories.hed: Deb Haaland Wins Historic New Mexico Senate Primary
stories.dek: Former Interior Secretary Deb Haaland secured a landmark Democratic primary win in New Mexico, a race carrying enormous significance for Native communities across the country. History was made — quietly, while everyone else watched the missiles.

stories.hed: Kennedy Center Drops 'Trump' Branding as Bill Maher's Twain Award Guest List Drops
stories.dek: The Kennedy Center quietly removed Trump-era branding as the Twain Prize for American Humor prepares its guest list under Bill Maher. Nothing says 'comedy' quite like a Washington institution quietly redacting itself.

markets.context: Stocks bounced back Monday as Iran paused its strikes on Israel and chip stocks clawed back last week's brutal selloff, while oil held elevated gains and gold slipped as the war-premium cooled slightly.

gas.note: At $4.17/gal, Americans are paying $1.21 more than before the Iran war started — the Strait of Hormuz is closed but your wallet is wide open.

popCulture.hed: 2026 Tony Awards: Pink Hosts Broadway's Biggest Night, Aubrey Plaza Makes Her Red Carpet Couple Debut
popCulture.dek: Pink took the Radio City Music Hall stage as first-time Tony host on June 7, while Aubrey Plaza and Christopher Abbott made their official debut as a couple — baby on the way and all. Broadway came through harder than any ceasefire this week.

genZ.definition: A TikTok meme rooted in the dramatic saxophone score from the 1991 film Boyz n the Hood, used to signal that a bad or unavoidable outcome is creeping closer. The format layers the swelling sax audio over any situation that looks fine on the surface but is clearly spiraling — your gut knows before your brain does.
genZ.usage: Israel and Iran signing a ceasefire in April... and the saxophones getting louder every single week since.

tldr: On the 100th day of the Iran-Israel war, both sides exchanged missile strikes before pausing fire under pressure from Trump, while Apple held its most watched WWDC ever as Tim Cook said goodbye and introduced a Gemini-powered Siri that might actually work this time. Meanwhile, a devastating earthquake hit the Philippines, stocks rebounded, and gas remains $1.21 more expensive than it was before anyone knew what Operation Epic Fury was.
